macro_context.major_event: US-Israel military strike on Iran (Feb 28, 2026). Strait of Hormuz closed. Crude at $104–122/bbl. India importing 90% crude. Government slashed fuel excise by ₹10/L. FIIs net sold ₹1.04 lakh crore in FY26 YTD.
macro_context.market_holiday: March 26 (Ram Navami). March 28 Saturday — no trading.

DVR LTP estimated at ~40% disc to regular

signals.title: Crude Oil Shock → MRPL Earnings Under Severe Pressure
signals.data_source: Brent crude at $104–$122/bbl (CNN Business, Al Jazeera Mar 26); MRPL LTP ₹181.65 (Upstox); India imports 90% crude (Anand Rathi PMS)
signals.reasoning: MRPL is a public-sector oil refiner. Crude oil is its primary input cost. With Brent at $104–$122 (vs ~$70 pre-Iran war), MRPL's gross refining margins (GRM) are being compressed despite India's fuel tax cuts. India slashed excise by ₹10/L to protect consumers, meaning MRPL cannot pass on cost increases. Current LTP ₹181.65 vs avg ₹196.40 already -7.5%. Further downside expected in Q4 FY26 results.
signals.action: Do NOT add. Monitor Q4 earnings for GRM data. Stop loss zone: ₹160.
signals.risk_to_thesis: Crude falls sharply if Iran-US ceasefire agreed; India government provides refiner subsidies

signals.title: SWIGGY Near 52-Week Low — Breach Imminent Risk
signals.data_source: SWIGGY LTP ₹268.55, 52-wk low ₹266.30 (ICICI Direct); -36.97% in 6 months; market cap ₹74,128 Cr
signals.reasoning: SWIGGY is 3.2% above its 52-week low of ₹266.30. The stock has fallen 36.97% in 6 months. Quick commerce war with Zomato/Blinkit, Instamart, Zepto continues. Path to profitability remains uncertain. User has combined 382 shares across both portfolios (170 ICICI avg ₹298.75 + 212 Groww avg ₹375.28). A breach of the 52-week low would trigger further stop-loss selling and technical breakdown.
signals.action: URGENT: Define stop-loss. If SWIGGY closes below ₹260, consider exiting ICICI position (avg ₹298.75 = -12.9% max loss). Groww position already -28.4% — evaluate exit vs hold.
signals.risk_to_thesis: Q4 earnings show profitability surprise; GMV growth accelerates; competitor pulls back

signals.title: INDIGO: ATF Cost Explosion from Crude Spike
signals.data_source: ATF tracks Brent crude. Brent at $104–$122 (CNN, CNBC Mar 2026). INDIGO LTP ₹4,294.70 vs avg ₹4,204.69 (+2.1%). Fuel = ~35–40% of airline operating costs.
signals.reasoning: Aviation turbine fuel (ATF) is priced on crude oil benchmarks and typically comprises 35–40% of IndiGo's operating cost. Brent crude surge from $70 to $104–$122 per barrel directly translates to a ~49–75% ATF cost spike. While India has cut petrol/diesel excise, ATF pricing reform is a separate process. IndiGo may hedge partially but Q4/Q1FY27 margins will be under severe pressure. The +2.1% current gain is misleading — Q4 results risk severe miss.
signals.action: Hold but monitor for Q4 earnings date. Do NOT add. If crude sustains above $100, consider partial exit above ₹4,300.
signals.risk_to_thesis: Crude falls; IndiGo has fuel hedges in place; ATF excise cut by government

signals.title: FII Sustained Outflow + PMI Weakness = Nifty Downside Risk
signals.data_source: FIIs: -₹3.15T FY26, -₹1.04L cr CY26 (Business Standard Mar 27). March 23 alone: FIIs -₹10,000 cr. India PMI lowest since Oct 2022 (HSBC flash). Nifty close: 22,819.60 (-2.09% Mar 27). Sensex -1,690 pts.
signals.reasoning: Three converging bearish forces: (1) Persistent FII selling with no reversal signal — analysts see 'modest flows returning only in H2 FY27'. (2) India PMI slowed to lowest in 3.5 years. (3) Geopolitical uncertainty from Iran war with no ceasefire in sight as of March 27. DIIs are supporting markets but cannot fully offset FII scale of selling. Nifty has corrected from highs and sits at ~22,820 — further downside to 22,000–22,200 range is the base case.
signals.action: Increase cash allocation opportunity. Avoid leveraged positions. Watch 22,500 Nifty support.
signals.risk_to_thesis: Iran ceasefire; Fed rate cuts; FII rotation back to EMs; India Q4 GDP beat

signals.title: ASTRA MICRO +83% — Partial Profit Booking Opportunity
signals.data_source: ASTRA MICRO LTP ~₹1,030 vs avg ₹562.35 (Groww portfolio). 20 shares held.
signals.reasoning: Astra Microwave Products is a defence electronics company riding the dual tailwinds of India's increasing defence budgets and heightened global defence spending post-Iran war. The stock has more than doubled. At 20 shares, total unrealized P&L ≈ ₹9,353 (+₹46,730 gain). Given stretched defence sector valuations (Nifty Defence at 50.6x TTM PE vs 10-yr avg 36.6x) and the small position size, booking 50% (10 shares) locks in meaningful gains while retaining upside exposure.
signals.action: Consider selling 10 of 20 shares at market open Monday. Book ₹4,676 profit. Hold remaining 10 with stop loss at ₹850.
signals.risk_to_thesis: Iran war intensifies further; additional large defence orders announced; election spending

signals.title: PSU Infrastructure Cluster: IRFC / IREDA / IRCON / NHPC — No Near-Term Catalyst
signals.data_source: IRFC ~₹90 (est. -30.8%); IREDA ₹114.80 (-34.4% Groww avg); IRCON ₹115.61 (-28.7% Groww avg); NHPC ₹76.82 (-26.3%); Budget 2026 commentary (Business Standard, Goodreturns); India fiscal squeeze from oil shock (CNBC Mar 27).
signals.reasoning: This cluster of PSU infrastructure stocks represents the portfolio's largest P&L drag. The oil shock has created a fiscal emergency for India — government slashed fuel excise by ₹10/L ('huge hit to tax revenue' per CNBC). This fiscal squeeze directly reduces discretionary capital expenditure on railways, renewables, and infrastructure, which are the primary revenue sources for IRFC, IREDA, IRCON, and NHPC. IRCON and IREDA bear the double burden of compressed government capex + elevated borrowing costs from inflation. No near-term re-rating catalyst visible until: (a) oil prices normalize, or (b) FY27 Q1 government spending data shows recovery.
signals.action: DO NOT AVERAGE DOWN. Hold with patience. Set calendar reminder for FY27 Budget capex tracking (post-April). NHPC+ACME BESS deal is a medium-term positive for NHPC.
signals.risk_to_thesis: Iran ceasefire + crude correction → government fiscal relief → capex resumes

signals.title: COAL INDIA +33% — Consider Partial Profit Booking
signals.data_source: Coal India LTP ₹462.25 vs avg ₹345.55 (Groww). 55 shares. Oil shock of 2026 increasing coal demand as substitute fuel.
signals.reasoning: Coal India is a beneficiary of the oil shock — industrial users are substituting expensive LNG/oil with coal. The +33.8% gain on 55 shares = ~₹6,413 unrealized profit. However, the clean energy transition and NHPC/Solar expansion remain structural headwinds. JM Financial has a buy on Coal India in the power stocks basket. The stock has performed well but government pricing controls cap upside. Consider booking 25–50% of position at these levels.
signals.action: Consider selling 25–30 shares at current ₹462 levels. Book ~₹3,000 profit. Hold remaining for coal demand tailwind.
signals.risk_to_thesis: Iran war ends; oil normalizes; coal demand drops; clean energy accelerates

signals.title: IT Sector Gap — Portfolio Lacks Defensive USD-Earning Exposure
signals.data_source: User has zero IT exposure. Portfolio scan confirms no IT stock. Nifty IT is a USD-earnings sector (defensive vs INR depreciation). Iran war → INR depreciation risk (HSBC, Bloomberg Mar 2026).
signals.reasoning: With Brent at $104–$122, India's current account deficit is widening significantly (India imports ~$100B+ of crude annually). This puts depreciation pressure on INR vs USD. IT companies (TCS, Infosys, HCL, Wipro) earn in USD — INR depreciation directly boosts their INR revenues. Additionally, IT sector is not affected by crude prices, FII selling has disproportionately hit PSU/infra stocks, and IT valuations are relatively cheaper ('cheap' per 2026 investment strategy report). This is a persistent portfolio gap.
signals.action: Once market stabilizes (Nifty finding support), consider initiating small position in Nifty IT via ETF (MIRAE/KOTAK NIFTY IT ETF) as a portfolio hedge and diversifier. Target: 5–8% portfolio allocation.
signals.risk_to_thesis: US recession tanks IT spending; USD weakens; rupee appreciates

signals.title: SBI Gold Fund + SETFGOLD — Verify ETF Price vs Gold ATH
signals.data_source: Gold at $5,300/oz ATH (Intellectia AI); Gold at ₹14,689/gram in India (BusinessToday Mar 27). SETFGOLD data shows ₹121.42 vs avg ₹125.21 (-3%). Gold ETF AUM up 165% YoY (BusinessToday).
signals.reasoning: Gold has hit an all-time high of $5,300/oz — driven by Iran war safe-haven demand. India's domestic gold price at ₹14,689/gram is also elevated. However, SETFGOLD shows ₹121.42 (search result) which implies a -3% loss vs avg ₹125.21. This apparent discrepancy may be due to: (a) stale ETF price data, (b) the user bought SETFGOLD when gold was near current highs, or (c) technical factors. SBI Gold Fund SIP (₹15K/mo) should be doing very well given gold's trajectory. ACTION: Verify current SETFGOLD price on ICICI Direct portal. The hedge is working — gold is the portfolio's structural safe haven.
signals.action: Verify SETFGOLD price on broker app. Continue SBI Gold Fund SIP — this is a strong performer. Consider increasing SIP amount given geopolitical risk outlook.
signals.risk_to_thesis: Iran-US de-escalation; strong dollar keeps gold capped; Fed hawkishness

signals.title: ADANI PORT — Strait of Hormuz + Adani Overhang
signals.data_source: Adani Port LTP ₹1,375.90 vs avg ₹1,579 (-12.9%). Strait of Hormuz closed since Mar 4 (Wikipedia). India-Middle East shipping disrupted.
signals.reasoning: Adani Ports (Mundra) is India's largest port. Two headwinds: (1) Strait of Hormuz closure has disrupted India-Middle East trade, a significant volume for Mundra. Ships are rerouting via Cape of Good Hope, reducing throughput and increasing transit times. (2) Adani Group overhang from previous controversy persists in investor sentiment. At -12.9% vs avg, the position is already in pain. The trade disruption risk is real and could persist until Hormuz reopens. No near-term catalyst visible.
signals.action: Do NOT add. Hold and monitor Hormuz re-opening news as key catalyst. Stop loss at ₹1,250.
signals.risk_to_thesis: Hormuz reopens; Middle East de-escalation; Adani Group positive catalyst; India-US trade diversion

signals.title: HDFC Defence Fund — Strong Performer, Valuations Stretched
signals.data_source: HDFC Defence Fund NAV ₹23.657 (Groww, Mar 27). 1-yr return: 21%. Since launch: 136.36%. Nifty India Defence at 50.6x TTM PE (Business Standard). User invested ₹3,14,984.
signals.reasoning: The HDFC Defence Fund is the portfolio's best-performing asset at +21% 1-yr return (+22% estimated on user's ₹3.14L investment). Iran war is a fundamental tailwind — India's defence spending is rising and global defence budgets are expanding. However, Nifty India Defence trades at 50.6x TTM PE vs 10-yr average 36.6x — valuations are significantly stretched. The new ICICI SIP in Regular plan starting April 18 is fine for long-term averaging but avoid lump-sum additions at current valuations.
signals.action: Hold existing Groww investment. Proceed with April 18 SIP start (Regular plan ICICI). Do NOT make lump-sum additions at current 50x PE levels. Book partial profits if NAV crosses ₹27+.
signals.risk_to_thesis: Defence orders delayed; budget allocation disappoints; Iran ceasefire reduces geopolitical premium

signals.title: TATA MOTORS DVR — Structural Discount Unlikely to Narrow
signals.data_source: Tata Motors LTP ₹427.60 (Business Standard Mar 27). DVR estimated ~₹290–₹320 (40-50% discount standard). Avg cost DVR: ₹622.74. Loss: ~-49 to -51%.
signals.reasoning: This is the portfolio's deepest red position (-51%). DVR (Differential Voting Rights) shares trade at a structural 40-50% discount to regular shares because they have lower voting rights. Tata Motors regular shares have recovered strongly (+51.8% for Groww regular shares). But DVR will always trade at a discount. Unless Tata Motors announces conversion/delisting of DVR (as some companies have done), the absolute loss in DVR will remain. The path to recovery requires: (1) massive bull run in Tata Motors regular, AND (2) DVR discount narrowing — both unlikely simultaneously.
signals.action: Do NOT average down DVR. Consider exiting Tata Motors DVR on any sharp rally (above ₹350). Redeploy capital into regular Tata Motors or other quality stocks if exiting. File capital loss for tax offset.
signals.risk_to_thesis: Tata Motors announces DVR buyback or conversion; JLR delivers exceptional results; DVR discount narrows below 30%

signals.title: ACME SOLAR-NHPC BESS Deal — Medium-Term Positive
signals.data_source: NHPC-ACME Solar signed BESS deal: 250 MW FDRE PPA (Renewable Watch Feb 2026); BESS deal for Andhra Pradesh (EQ Magazine). ACME Solar LTP ₹254.63 (-11.9%); NHPC ₹76.82 (-26.3%).
signals.reasoning: ACME Solar and NHPC have inked a landmark Battery Energy Storage Systems deal to power Andhra Pradesh's energy future. This represents a step-change in India's renewable infrastructure build-out and creates a long-term revenue stream. The BESS capacity of 275 MW/550 MWh positions both companies in the growing energy storage market. However, the immediate impact on stock prices is limited as government capex is constrained by the oil shock. This is a 2–3 year story.
signals.action: Hold. This is a positive fundamental development. No immediate action required. Monitor government capex restoration as a trigger.
signals.risk_to_thesis: Project delays; government cancels/defers; competition from other BESS providers

signals.title: JIOFIN — Pre-Revenue Stage, Watch for Asset Management Launch
signals.data_source: JIOFIN LTP ₹237.35 (Mar 25). ICICI avg ₹234.99 (+1%), Groww avg ₹238.30 (-0.4%). Combined 520 shares.
signals.reasoning: Jio Financial Services is effectively at breakeven across both portfolios. The company is building its financial services ecosystem (asset management, insurance, lending, payments). Key catalysts: formal AMC launch, insurance business scaling, Jio Finance app traction. The stock is range-bound as the market waits for revenue visibility. No immediate action needed but position needs a 12–18 month horizon to realize upside.
signals.action: Hold. Monitor AMC launch announcement and quarterly updates for signs of monetization.
signals.risk_to_thesis: Regulatory delays in financial licenses; competitive pressure from HDFC AMC, SBI MF; macro headwinds
